ddls:
CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic entry */,
  "sex" INTEGER /* Sex (0 for male, 1 for female) */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age group */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of data collection */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population count */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the geographical region */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or remarks */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_age FOREIGN KEY ("age") REFERENCES ages ("age_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_year FOREIGN KEY ("year") REFERENCES years ("year_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_sex FOREIGN KEY ("sex") REFERENCES sexes ("sex_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ages" (
  "age_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each age group */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Description of the age group */,
  "age_range" TEXT /* Range of the age group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("age_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sexes" (
  "sex_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sex category */,
  "sex_type" TEXT /* Description of the sex category (male or female) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the sex category */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sex_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "years" (
  "year_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each year */,
  "year_value" INTEGER /* Actual year value */,
  "quarter" INTEGER /* Quarter of the year */,
  "month" INTEGER /* Month of the year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "population_changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each population change */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic entry */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of data collection */,
  "change_value" INTEGER /* Change in population */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (birth, death, migration) */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_population_changes_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "growth_rates" (
  "growth_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each growth rate */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic entry */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of data collection */,
  "growth_rate" REAL /* Growth rate of population */,
  "method" TEXT /* Method used to calculate the growth rate */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("growth_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_growth_rates_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, data scientist, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user (active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic entry being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the demographic entry */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the demographic entry was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic entry */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date of the data version */,
  "version_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the data version */,
  "version_type" TEXT /* Type of the data version (major, minor) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id")
)
